title: A decade after her father’s death, 24-year-old runs the Boston Marathon for grief support
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Violet Morgan, 24, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her late father, John Morgan.
Morgan chose to support The Children's Room, a nonprofit offering grief support to families experiencing loss.
Inspired by her father's memory, Morgan fell in love with running and aims to give back through the marathon.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 18)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington sold a one-family ranch on Rice St. for $460,000, built in 1951 on a 7,235-square-foot lot.
Andover's highest sale was a one-family antique at 53 Salem St. for $2,640,000 with 5,407 square feet.
In Boston, a condominium at 89 Beacon St. sold for $17,000,000, featuring 15 rooms and 8 baths.

title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins' power play ranks 28th post-Olympic break with a 12.8% success rate, a decline from third in the NHL before.
Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm have zero points in 5-on-5 play during last 11 games, impacting offensive production.
Andrew Peeke and Nikita Zadorov's pairing has been outscored 4-2 in 96 minutes since returning from the Olympic break.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit a decisive home run in Venezuela's 3-2 win over Team USA, securing the World Baseball Classic title.
Former Red Sox pitcher Eduardo Rodriguez contributed with over four scoreless innings, aiding Venezuela's historic championship win.
Red Sox players Jarren Duran and Masataka Yoshida had strong performances in the tournament for Mexico and Japan, respectively.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan from Ashland is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
The center supports children with complex medical needs and provides relief to their families.
Flanagan began running three years ago and finds it beneficial for her mental and physical health.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker were found guilty of involuntary manslaughter in the death of their foster child.
The 10-month-old foster child died from a treatable strep infection and bronchopneumonia due to lack of medical care.
The couple faces up to 20 years for manslaughter and 2.5 years for reckless child endangerment, sentencing on March 19.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A water main break in Jackson Square flooded streets at Columbus Avenue and Centre and Ritchie streets in Jamaica Plain.
Boston police rerouted traffic while MBTA detoured routes for buses 14, 22, 29, 41, and 44; the Orange Line was unaffected.
Three daycare businesses evacuated due to flooding; repairs to shut off the main are ongoing, affecting a large area beyond Egleston Square.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Waddle was traded to the Broncos for a first-round pick and two additional 2026 draft selections.
The trade complicates the Patriots' pursuit of A.J. Brown, as it sets a high price for top receivers.
Broncos improve their roster, impacting the Patriots' competitive position in the AFC after a strong 2025 season.

title: 2 charged after car hits pedestrians outside of downtown Boston nightclub
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Darius Boyce and Maria Moraes face charges after hitting three pedestrians with a Jeep outside ICON nightclub.
Boyce charged with assault and felony weapon counts; Moraes charged with interfering with police and disorderly conduct.
Victims identified Boyce and Moraes; police found a loaded gun in the Jeep after a traffic stop.

title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Joe Biden made a surprise appearance at the Irish American Partnership's St. Patrick's Day Breakfast in Boston.
Biden discussed the immigrant experiences of his Irish ancestors and modern challenges amid immigration crackdowns in his speech.
Governor Maura Healey also spoke, highlighting Boston's connection to Irish American heritage and reflecting on immigrant ancestors' experiences.
